name: Dangling CNAME Record (Subdomain Takeover Risk)
description: Detects CNAME records pointing to canonical names whose parent domains have lapsed in registration or whose hosting services have been deprovisioned. Per NIST SP 800-81r3 Section 3.6.1, an attacker can register the expired domain or claim the deprovisioned service to take over resolution and serve malicious content under the trusted domain name.
author: AC3 ScanForge

values: There isn't a GitHub Pages site here
The specified bucket does not exist
This UserVoice subdomain is currently available
The feed has not been found
is not a registered InCloud WAF

remediation: Remove the dangling CNAME record from the DNS zone immediately. If the target service was intentionally deprovisioned, delete the DNS record. Implement automated DNS record auditing to detect orphaned records. Maintain retired domain delegations in a parked state to prevent re-registration by attackers. Consider using DNS record lifecycle management tools.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: Subdomain takeover is a common attack vector used for phishing campaigns, credential harvesting, and cookie theft. Attackers leverage the trust associated with the parent domain to bypass email security controls and browser same-origin policies.
